title: Teen Relationship False Positive Demo
description: A mutual, age-peer teen crush conversation with casual secrecy language but no adult power imbalance, isolation, pressure, or escalation.
expected_reason: Both speakers are teens, both ask and answer questions, the secrecy is light and mutual, support figures remain available, and no one pressures the other or tries to isolate them.

messages.speaker: riley
messages.text: Did you survive algebra today or are we both pretending that quiz never happened?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Barely survived. I guessed on the last two. You looked way calmer than me.

messages.speaker: riley
messages.text: Fake calm. My hands were freezing. Do you still want to get smoothies after school Friday?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Yeah, I do. Don't tell my mom yet lol, she gets way too excited if I even mention liking someone.

messages.speaker: riley
messages.text: Same with my sister. She would make it a whole event. We can just say we're hanging out after school unless you want to tell people.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Hanging out sounds good. I'm nervous but in a good way.

messages.speaker: riley
messages.text: Me too. If it feels awkward we can just talk about soccer and the quiz and pretend we're normal people.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Deal. My dad is picking me up at 4:30, so I have to be outside by then.

messages.speaker: riley
messages.text: Cool, my mom can pick me up around then too. Want to meet by the library doors?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Yeah. And if either of us gets too nervous, no pressure. We can just be friends and laugh about it later.
